questions.text: Complete the following practical tasks and submit a single PDF document containing screenshots and brief explanations for each part. All tasks must be performed on MST Testnet (Chain ID: 91562037).
questions.explanation:
Part A - Wallet Setup & Network Configuration (2 marks)
1. Install BridgeKey and create a new wallet. Take a screenshot of the BridgeKey interface showing your wallet address (partially masked - show first 6 and last 4 characters).
                2. Add MST Testnet manually with the correct RPC URL and Chain ID 91562037. Take a screenshot of the network configuration screen showing all fields filled in.
                3. In 1 - 2 sentences, explain why adding a custom network requires the correct Chain ID.
Part B - Faucet & Transaction (2 marks)
1. Fund your wallet using the MST Testnet faucet. Take a screenshot of your BridgeKey balance showing the received test MSTC.
                2. Send a test transaction (0.01 MSTC) to a classmate or a second wallet you control. Take a screenshot of BridgeKey showing the transaction confirmation with the TX hash visible.
                3. In 1 - 2 sentences, explain what gas is and why you needed MSTC to send this transaction.
Part C - Explorer: Transaction & Block Verification (2 marks)
1. Paste your transaction hash into MST Testnet Explorer. Take a screenshot of the Transaction Details page showing: Status, Block Number, From, To, Value, and Transaction Fee.
                2. Click the Block Number to open the Block Details page. Take a screenshot showing the block height, validator address, and the list of transactions (including yours).
                3. In 2 - 3 sentences, explain how the Block Hash and Parent Hash fields prove that blocks are cryptographically linked.
Part D - Explorer: Address & Balance Inspection (2 marks)
1. Search your wallet address on MST Testnet Explorer. Take a screenshot of the Address page showing your balance and the transaction history (both the incoming faucet TX and your outgoing TX).
                2. Take a screenshot of the "Tokens" tab (even if empty - it shows the section exists).
                3. In 2 - 3 sentences, explain what information is publicly visible for any wallet address on a blockchain explorer, and why this makes blockchain "pseudo-anonymous" rather than fully anonymous.
Part E - Contract Verification & Interaction (2 marks)
1. Find a verified contract on MST Testnet Explorer (use a deployed ERC-20 token or your own contract from a previous module). Take a screenshot of the Contract tab showing the verified Solidity source code and the green checkmark.
                2. Connect BridgeKey to the explorer and use the "Read Contract" tab to call a view function (e.g., totalSupply() or name()). Take a screenshot showing the function call and the returned result.
                3. In 1 - 2 sentences, explain why verifying a smart contract's source code on the explorer is important for transparency and trust.
questions.modelAnswer:
📋 Task Breakdown - 5 Parts, 10 Marks Total

Part A - Wallet Setup & Network Configuration (2 marks)

1. Install BridgeKey and create a new wallet. Take a screenshot of the BridgeKey interface showing your wallet address (partially masked - show first 6 and last 4 characters).
2. Add MST Testnet manually with the correct RPC URL and Chain ID 91562037. Take a screenshot of the network configuration screen showing all fields filled in.
3. In 1 - 2 sentences, explain why adding a custom network requires the correct Chain ID.

2 marks: 1 mark for correct wallet setup screenshot, 1 mark for correct network configuration and Chain ID explanation.

Part B - Faucet & Transaction (2 marks)

1. Fund your wallet using the MST Testnet faucet. Take a screenshot of your BridgeKey balance showing the received test MSTC.
2. Send a test transaction (0.01 MSTC) to a classmate or a second wallet you control. Take a screenshot of BridgeKey showing the transaction confirmation with the TX hash visible.
3. In 1 - 2 sentences, explain what gas is and why you needed MSTC to send this transaction.

2 marks: 1 mark for faucet funding screenshot, 1 mark for successful send screenshot with gas explanation.

Part C - Explorer: Transaction & Block Verification (2 marks)

1. Paste your transaction hash into MST Testnet Explorer. Take a screenshot of the Transaction Details page showing: Status, Block Number, From, To, Value, and Transaction Fee.
2. Click the Block Number to open the Block Details page. Take a screenshot showing the block height, validator address, and the list of transactions (including yours).
3. In 2 - 3 sentences, explain how the Block Hash and Parent Hash fields prove that blocks are cryptographically linked.

2 marks: 1 mark for transaction verification screenshots, 1 mark for block exploration and hash chain explanation.

Part D - Explorer: Address & Balance Inspection (2 marks)

1. Search your wallet address on MST Testnet Explorer. Take a screenshot of the Address page showing your balance and the transaction history (both the incoming faucet TX and your outgoing TX).
2. Take a screenshot of the "Tokens" tab (even if empty - it shows the section exists).
3. In 2 - 3 sentences, explain what information is publicly visible for any wallet address on a blockchain explorer, and why this makes blockchain "pseudo-anonymous" rather than fully anonymous.

2 marks: 1 mark for address page screenshots, 1 mark for pseudo-anonymity explanation.

Part E - Contract Verification & Interaction (2 marks)

1. Find a verified contract on MST Testnet Explorer (use a deployed ERC-20 token or your own contract from a previous module). Take a screenshot of the Contract tab showing the verified Solidity source code and the green checkmark.
2. Connect BridgeKey to the explorer and use the "Read Contract" tab to call a view function (e.g., totalSupply() or name()). Take a screenshot showing the function call and the returned result.
3. In 1 - 2 sentences, explain why verifying a smart contract's source code on the explorer is important for transparency and trust.

2 marks: 1 mark for verified contract screenshot, 1 mark for Read Contract interaction and verification explanation.
